title: hieracium saxifragum subsp. extensiforme
intro: Hieracium saxifragum subsp. extensiforme, also known as hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Traditionally, hawkweed has been prepared as a tea or infusion, and its medicinal properties have been utilized through topical applications, such as poultices and salves.
